F: Redis prod @ redis-prod-a.internal:6380; TLS enabled; maxmemory=4GB; eviction=allkeys-lru
F: replica=redis-prod-b.internal; async replication; lag~<50ms
F: backups→offsite object store; nightly@03:15 UTC
D: RDB disabled; AOF off ∵ write latency target p99<1ms; no disk persistence on primary

F: postgres cluster=db-prod-01; version=16.2; OS=Ubuntu 24.04
F: data_dir=/var/lib/postgresql/16/main; WAL archiving=on; archive_command→/mnt/backup/pg-wal/
F: shared_buffers=8GB; effective_cache_size=24GB
F: max_connections=200; pgbouncer@port=6432; mode=transaction pooling

D: Mnemos vector store → sqlite-vec (chosen 2026-02-13, v6 planning) ∵ embedded, single-writer, zero-deployment default; Qdrant available as optional backend ∵ multi-node/higher QPS needs
✗ pgvector, Chroma, Weaviate — all rejected ∵ require separate server process

D: 2026-02-15 v7.0 △ embedding model Ollama Qwen3 → FastEmbed multilingual-e5-large ∵ Qwen3 4x slower on CPU; poor Swedish handling (prod memory store requires it); e5-large wins on speed despite Qwen3 slightly higher raw cosine on English
F: multilingual-e5-large; 1024-dim; ONNX format; CPU-native; ~120 passages/sec @ Ryzen 7 8-core desktop

⚠ W: NEVER run db:migrate:fresh on production; no safeguard trigger exists there
F: staging box test_shop has trigger prevent_fresh → raises exception on db:migrate:fresh
W: prior incident: 12hrs customer messages lost ∵ db:migrate:fresh ran with wrong --env flag
D: always verify APP_ENV before any migrate command

F: Jina rerank-v2 cross-encoder; trained on query-document pairs; outputs raw logits (not calibrated probabilities)
F: usable score = sigmoid(raw_logit); sigmoid(raw) >= 0.55 empirically correlates with "actual duplicate"; below 0.55 = noise
F: threshold stored as DEDUP_RERANK_THRESHOLD @ mnemos.constants

L: topic-level merge clusters (cosine 0.75–0.88) need more aggressive guardrails than near-duplicate clusters (>0.88) ∵ LLM over-generalizes/drops specifics on loosely-related inputs
L: hierarchical binary merge (introduced v10) mitigates this → LLM merges only 2 memories at a time ∴ "output ~1 input size" intuition holds

W: qdrant API key rotation procedure (6 steps)
F: step1→ gen key: `openssl rand -hex 32`
F: step2→ write to `/etc/qdrant/config.yaml` under `service.api_key`
F: step3→ `systemctl restart qdrant`
F: step4→ update `MNEMOS_QDRANT_API_KEY` in `/etc/mnemos/env`
F: step5→ `systemctl restart mnemos-mcp`
F: step6→ verify: `curl -H 'api-key: NEW' http://localhost:6333/collections`
W: old key remains valid until rotation completes

F: Mnemos LongMemEval benchmark; dataset=longmemeval_s.json (HuggingFace), cleaned; non-abstention Q=470; full set=500Q
F: R@5 hybrid+rerank w/o CML=98.94%; w/ CML=99.15%
F: end-to-end QA accuracy=77.4% (answerer=Sonnet, judge=Opus, full 500Q set)
F: all numbers reproducible from scripts @ benchmarks/

F: FastEmbed/e5-large cold-start ~9s; RAM ~2.1GB; inference ~120 passages/s @ Ryzen 7 8-core/ONNX CPU
F: warm-up @ MCP server start → first memory_search post-boot ~15ms (model pre-loaded in memory)

The webhook secret was suspected as potentially wrong during initial debugging.
per_memory.cml_output:
W: Stripe webhook signature verification fails if raw request body not preserved byte-for-byte from wire
F: root cause → FastAPI middleware was reading body stream before webhook handler; stream empty/parsed by time Stripe hashed it
F: fix → disable body parsing on `/webhooks/stripe` route specifically via dependency override
D: document this fix ∵ high forget-risk; discovered after ~3-4 failed attempts + ~1hr additional digging
L: Stripe requires raw request body for signature verification; parsed/buffered body breaks it

D: keep sqlite-vec as default vector store for Mnemos; ship pgvector as optional backend later
∵ zero-deployment is a feature for single-user memory system; sqlite-vec lives in same SQLite file as memory data; sqlite-vec has been stable; no multi-node vector search needed at current scale
∵ pgvector argument: Postgres already running for app data, maintaining separate embedded index felt redundant
F: Mnemos designed as single-user memory system
F: vector store options evaluated: sqlite-vec (current default) ↔ pgvector (future optional backend)

F: embedding pipeline benchmarks; ref hw=Ryzen 7 8-core desktop, 64GB ECC RAM
F: FastEmbed loading multilingual-e5-large; cold-start load time ~9s; occurs once per MCP server boot
F: model resident RAM once loaded ~2.1GB
F: inference throughput ~120 passages/sec; measured batch=100 short passages; CPU-native ONNX runtime
F: first memory_search latency after startup ~15ms; model warm in memory via warmup hook in MCP server

⚠ W: ran `db:migrate:fresh` on production instead of staging; date=Jan 22; site=example-shop.com
F: cause→APP_ENV flag pointed at production, not staging
F: data loss~12hrs of customer messages
F: staging has trigger `prevent_fresh` (raises exception on destructive migration); production does NOT have this trigger ∴ production executed silently
D: hard rule→always read APP_ENV + verify target hostname before running `db:migrate:fresh`; no exceptions for fatigue/urgency
